steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content:
The passage opens by saying Nixon is most famous for Watergate, then shifts with the word But to declare his legacy is complex with actual achievements in the EPA and healthcare reform.

This But is the defining signal: it frames the main idea as a contrast between his public reputation (Watergate scandal) and his unrecognized accomplishments.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the answer
steps.content:
Choice B captures the core contrast: Nixon is “commonly linked with an infamous historical event” but this “overshadows some of his notable achievements.” The other three either treat a supporting detail as the main idea (A: the people involved in Watergate; C: his influence on later administrations) or misread what caused his reputation (D claims his associates shaped it, when the passage states he had his own “participation”).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
